channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: A Receipt
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Kristie Bailey/The New York Times

item.title: Home Health Care Aides Say It’s Time to End ‘Inhumane’ 24-Hour Shifts
item.description: The aides want the New York City Council to to pass a law that would limit their shifts to 12 hours, except in the case of emergencies.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Parnell

media:content.media:description: Home health workers demonstrated on Wednesday at City Hall, calling for better working conditions.

item.title: The Wealthy House Candidate With a History of Bawdy Facebook Posts
item.description: The social media habits of Peter Chatzky, a tech executive who is running to unseat Representative Mike Lawler in New York, show a penchant for crude jokes.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:description: Peter Chatzky’s Facebook posts included jokes about sperm.

item.title: E.U. Loan for Ukraine Becomes Election Leverage for Orban
item.description: European leaders are pushing Prime Minister Viktor Orban to stop blocking a €90 billion loan to Ukraine. But the issue is a rallying cry in remarkably close elections in Hungary set for April 12.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes, Jeanna Smialek and Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary in Budapest on Sunday. His disputes with President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine have become a focal point of Hungary’s coming election.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 19, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Qatar Reports More Attacks on Energy Infrastructure as Oil Prices Rise
item.description: Qatar blamed Tehran for strikes on its natural gas facilities, a day after Iran’s largest natural gas source was hit. The price of oil climbed to $112 a barrel as uncertainty over the war’s impacts on energy supplies grew.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Tells the Tale of Dueling Senators
item.description: “Also very funny to describe a duel as being between two consenting adults. You know, because if only one person consents, that’s murder,” said Seth Meyers, host of “Late Night.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘immaterial’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 66 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: immaterial
item.description: This word has appeared in 66 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Adorable polizón: el zorro rojo que cruzó el Atlántico
item.description: Tras su increíble viaje a través del Atlántico, el peludo ahora está en cuarentena mientras disfruta de un menú de manzanas, camote y ratones en espera de un hogar permanente.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan
item.category: Port of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:description: Un zorro rojo encontrado a bordo de un carguero procedente de Inglaterra el mes pasado fue trasladado al zoológico del Bronx , donde se le sirve una dieta variada de frutas, verduras, insectos y ratones.

item.title: Israel’s Lebanese Campaign Will Backfire
item.description: A failure by Israel to engage with the Lebanese government in the fight against Hezbollah will undermine its own interests.
item.dc:creator: Shira Efron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: El Niño podría provocar fenómenos meteorológicos extremos pronto
item.description: Autoridades climáticas de EE. UU. advierten que hay una gran probabilidad de que el fenómeno, conocido por potenciar el calor global, llegue en agosto.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance

media:content.media:description: Una calle inundada en Cathedral City, California, en 2023. Los científicos han relacionado El Niño de 2023-24 con la sequía en el sur de África, los grandes incendios forestales en el Amazonas y las intensas lluvias en el sur de California.

item.title: Afroman Wins Civil Trial Over Use of Police Raid Footage in His Music Videos
item.description: Seven law enforcement officers had accused the rapper, whose name is Joseph E. Foreman, of causing them “humiliation” by using images from their 2022 raid on his Ohio home in two music videos.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:description: The rapper Afroman, whose real name is Joseph E. Foreman, performing in Hollywood, Fla., in 2018.

item.title: Oil Prices Keep Climbing on Heightened Fears Over Energy Supplies
item.description: Attacks on major energy facilities in Iran and Qatar injected new uncertainty into the outlook for oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: El ascenso de Mojtaba Jameneí en Irán
item.description: Pese a ser el hijo del líder previo, la llegada de Jameneí al poder estuvo plagada de luchas intestinas que enfrentaron a lo más conservador del sistema político iraní contra voces más moderadas.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una foto del nuevo líder supremo de Irán, Mojtaba Jamenei, en una ceremonia por los militares muertos por ataques aéreos estadounidenses e israelíes, en Teherán, Irán, la semana pasada.

item.title: In Swaths of Germany, the Far-Right AfD Is Part of the Fabric
item.description: The Alternative for Germany party, or AfD, is shunned in federal politics but is a regional force. This fall, it could win broader power for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Tatiana Firsova and Clay Risen

media:content.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Three AfD representatives — Christian Skotnik, left, Doreen Gärtner, center, and Enrico Schult — before a party meeting in Stavenhagen, Germany, in October.

item.title: The Iran War’s Economic Threat to Europe and Asia
item.description: Inflation and its consequences for growth are a growing concern for countries where memories of the 2022 energy crisis are fresh.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: A fuel station in Dhaka, Bangladesh, last week.

item.title: Ozempic Is About to Go Generic in India, China and Canada
item.description: In India, China and several other nations, Novo Nordisk is on the verge of losing patent protection for its blockbuster weight loss drug, opening the door for cheaper competing versions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins, Meaghan Tobin, Eshe Nelson, Alex Travelli and Pragati K.B.

media:content.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: March 19, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, March 19, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: A History of Conflicts That All Fell Short
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, March 19, 2026.

item.title: Joseph Duggar of ’19 Kids and Counting’ Faces Child Sex Abuse Charges
item.description: Mr. Duggar, a former star of the TLC reality show, was arrested in Arkansas and was awaiting extradition to Florida, where the authorities said he molested a 9-year-old girl in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Ida Mae Astute/Disney General Entertainment Content via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Joseph Duggar in 2016. Mr. Duggar, a former star of the TLC series “19 Kids and Counting,” was arrested in Arkansas on Wednesday, the authorities said.

item.title: F.B.I. Investigates Joe Kent, Whose Resignation Over Iran War Angered Trump
item.description: Mr. Kent is under investigation for a possible intelligence leak, according to people familiar with the situation. The inquiry is said to predate his resignation this week as the top U.S. counterterrorism official.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Joe Kent during a debate in Portland, Ore., in 2024.

item.title: Newsmax Executive Named as Voice of America’s Deputy
item.description: The appointment raised concerns that the Trump administration would try to erode the news group’s independence after picking a deputy who had been at conservative news channels for around 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: United States Agency for Global Media

media:content.media:description: The Trump administration last year halted most of the news operations of Voice of America, whose editorial independence has been mandated by Congress.

item.title: After Cesar Chavez Allegations, California Sex Abuse Laws Could Put U.F.W. at Financial Risk
item.description: Cesar Chavez’s labor union, United Farm Workers, could still face lawsuits for his sex abuse of girls and women, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler

media:content.media:description: Cesar Chavez has been celebrated by institutions across California, including San Jose State University, which has a mural of the labor leader.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 19, 2026
item.description: Center your mind to solve John Kugelman’s layered crossword.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Family of Minneapolis Boy Detained by ICE Faces Fast-Track Deportation
item.description: The family of 5-year-old Liam Conejos Ramos, who became a symbol of Trump’s immigration crackdown, is appealing their accelerated removal.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A photo of Liam Conejo Ramos was displayed during a Congressional hearing in Washington n February.

item.title: Bondi Doesn’t Commit to Deposition With House Panel Over Epstein Files
item.description: Under the rules of the oversight committee, Attorney General Pam Bondi received a subpoena requiring her to appear. The panel’s Republican chairman said he sent the summons reluctantly.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Attorney General Pam Bondi after a closed-door briefing for members of the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday.

item.title: Mullin Faces Questions About War Zone Observations
item.description: The nominee for homeland security secretary suggested that he had observed war firsthand but declined to provide details, which he said were “classified.”
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo
item.category: Senate Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs
House Committee on Energy and Commerce

media:content.media:description: Senator Markwayne Mullin was asked on Wednesday about comments he had made that suggested he had been in a war zone.

item.title: Iranian Missile Attack Leaves 3 Palestinians Dead in West Bank
item.description: Several others were injured in the strike, which hit a caravan being used as a hair salon in the town of Beit Awwa, according to Palestinian officials.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Palestinian security forces gathering at the scene where three Palestinian women were killed in an Iranian missile attack near Hebron in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Wednesday.

item.title: AI Replica of Val Kilmer to Appear in ‘As Deep as the Grave’
item.description: Kilmer’s estate collaborated with the filmmakers to make a digital likeness of the actor, who died of cancer last year.
item.dc:creator: Reggie Ugwu

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday
item.description: A day of strikes on energy facilities and testimony in Washington to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ras Laffan Industrial City, Qatar, earlier this month. A state-owned Qatari energy company said that Ras Laffan Industrial City had sustained “extensive damage” from missile attacks.

item.title: Senate G.O.P. Again Blocks Bid to Stop Iran War Until Trump Wins Authorization
item.description: For the second time since the war began, Senate Democrats tried and failed to win passage of a resolution that would have halted the offensive until President Trump went to Congress for approval.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An explosion in Tehran earlier this month. Since the United States and Israel began joint airstrikes against Iran, Democrats have demanded that the Trump administration share more information on the objectives, timeline and costs associated with the military campaign.

item.title: Trump’s D.H.S. Nominee Says He Would Approach Disaster Policy Differently From Noem
item.description: President Trump’s Homeland Security nominee, Senator Markwayne Mullin, Republican of Oklahoma, struck a softer tone at his confirmation hearing on Wednesday, reflecting the administration’s efforts to project a more moderate tone toward immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva and Jackeline Luna

item.title: Iran Turns Out for Funeral for Those Killed in Strikes
item.description: Some died in Iran, others on a sea far from home. They were honored together Wednesday at a procession in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Days of Anger and Fear for Iranians Living With Waves of Airstrikes
item.description: Residents in Tehran and elsewhere say the attacks come at all hours and rock the ground.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Apartments in a neighborhood of Tehran targeted by American-Israeli airstrikes this week.

item.title: Women in California Politics See Their Own Stories in Cesar Chavez Accusations
item.description: “I’ve seen the repercussions when you speak out against a man in power, and you are blamed for the repercussions,” a Los Angeles City Council member said.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan

media:content.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “The amount of weight — how heavy it must have been for Dolores Huerta to carry that for so long,” said Eunisses Hernandez, a Los Angeles City Council member.

item.title: FEMA Will Relaunch Climate Resiliency Grants
item.description: A judge ruled in December that the agency could not cancel a program that had helped states invest billions of dollars in disaster readiness.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance

media:content.media:description: Noel Boxer, an external affairs officer with FEMA, inspects the flow of raw sewage near the Potomac River in Cabin John, Md., on March 14, 2026.

item.title: Bear That Clawed 2 People in California Is Euthanized, Orphaning Its Cubs
item.description: Officials in Monrovia, Calif., had lobbied state wildlife officers to relocate the bear and her two cubs to the Angeles National Forest instead.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

item.title: N.Y.C. Teacher Is Accused of Having Child Sex Abuse Images and Meth Pipe
item.description: The teacher, Joseph Taylor McKeel, was arrested Wednesday morning as he was on his way to work.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar and Troy Closson

media:content.media:credit: Gabriele Holtermann-Gorden, Sipa, via Associated Press Images
media:content.media:description: The outside of the Department of Education on Court Street in Brooklyn, New York.

item.title: Cesar Chavez Inspired California’s Latino Leaders but Left a Complex Legacy
item.description: Latino politicians describe having been awed by Mr. Chavez’s work on behalf of farm workers. They are now devastated by the sexual abuse allegations against him.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Lauren Justice for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “I remember hearing him speak about the indignities that farm workers endure and the grape boycott movement,” said Antonio Villaraigosa, a former mayor of Los Angeles. “I was inspired by his quiet dignity and his seeming selflessness.”

item.title: Trump’s Planning for America’s 250th Emphasizes Religion’s Role in the Nation’s Founding
item.description: A closed-door White House event included news about the National Garden of American Heroes and an emphasis on the role of religion in the founding.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Schuessler and Zachary Small

item.title: California Probably Hasn’t Seen the Worst of This Week’s Heat Wave
item.description: Wednesday was the hottest March day ever in many cities across California, Nevada and Arizona. Phoenix recorded its earliest 100-degree day.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Santa Monica State Beach in California. The stifling heat across California and the Southwest is expected to reach its highest intensity on Friday.

item.title: NASA’s Hubble Telescope Spots Comet K1 Exploding Into Fragments
item.description: In a stroke of luck, astronomers saw the comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) break into four or five fragments in November after it passed close to the sun.
item.dc:creator: Robin George Andrews

media:content.media:credit: NASA, ESA, Dennis Bodewits (AU); Image Processing: Joseph DePasquale (STScI)

item.title: What’s a Threat? Gabbard Says It’s Up to Trump, on Iran and Elsewhere.
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, was left to square the president’s comments about an imminent nuclear threat from Iran with a letter from one of her trusted aides.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, during a Senate hearing into worldwide threats on Wednesday. She said only the president can decide what constitutes an imminent threat.

item.title: Following Trump, Republicans in Congress Propose to Ban Most Voting by Mail
item.description: A restrictive voter I.D. bill under consideration in the Senate could severely limit mail-in voting. Conservatives are pressing to end the practice outright, taking aim at an option that is widely used by voters.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Democrats are solidly opposed to the measure, and even some Republicans are balking at the added restrictions on voting by mail.

item.title: Serial Podcast: The Idiot
item.description: Compassion has its limits when it comes to your own cousin.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen, Daniel Guillemette, Andrey Borzenko and Lika Kremer of Libo/Libo Studios, Julie Snyder, Allison Leyton-Brown and Phoebe Wang

item.title: El Día de San Patricio tropical que celebra la historia africana
item.description: La isla de Montserrat concibe la festividad tanto como una celebración nacional como un hito más sombrío: la conmemoración de una rebelión de esclavos fallida.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and Luis Antonio Rojas

item.title: Nebraska Wildfires Consume Nearly 800,000 Acres
item.description: The Morrill fire, the largest wildfire in state history, was 16 percent contained on Wednesday. The blaze was blamed for the death of an 86-year-old woman, the governor said.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:credit: Kenneth Ferriera/Omaha World-Herald, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Firefighters responded on Thursday to a wildfire in Denton, Neb.

item.title: For Iranians, Bombing of Gas Field Worsens Already Dire Energy Crisis
item.description: The South Pars gas field, hit by airstrikes on Wednesday, is central to the energy supply of Iran, which was already suffering blackouts before the war.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Hiroko Tabuchi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The South Pars gas field, which Iran shares with Qatar, in 2019. The field accounts for as much as 70 percent of Iran’s gas production.

item.title: For Tschabalala Self, Art Is Romantic
item.description: The painter and sculptor discusses neighborhood murals, nonlinear storytelling and her commission for the New Museum, a 13-foot-tall rendering of a couple mid-embrace.
item.dc:creator: Gazelle Mba
